quiz.title: Calculus Differentiation Quiz 29
quiz.subtitle: Derivatives in Context — Motion, Rates, and Applications (20 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus
quiz.topic: First, Second, and Third Derivatives in Applied Contexts

questions.question: If $s(t) = 3t^2 - 5t + 2$ represents position (in meters), what is the velocity at $t=2$ seconds?
If $v(t) = 4t^3 - 2t$ represents velocity, what is the acceleration at $t=1$?
If $v(t) = t^2 - 4t + 3$, when is the object at rest?
questions.explanation: Object at rest when $v(t) = 0$: $(t-1)(t-3) = 0$, so $t = 1$ or $t = 3$

questions.question: If $s(t) = t^3 - 6t^2 + 9t$, what is the acceleration at $t=2$?
If $s(t) = t^4 - 4t^3$, when is acceleration zero?
If $v(t) = 2t - 6$, when is the object moving in the positive direction?
If $v(t) = t^2 - 4$ and $a(t) = 2t$, is the object speeding up or slowing down at $t=1$?
questions.explanation: At $t=1$: $v(1) = -3$ (negative), $a(1) = 2$ (positive). Signs differ, so slowing down

questions.question: If $s(t) = t^4$, what is the jerk (third derivative) at $t=1$?
If $v(t) = -t^2 + 6t$, when does maximum velocity occur?
If $V(t)$ represents volume of water in a tank, what does $V'(t)$ represent?
options: Rate of flow into/out of tank
questions.explanation: $V'(t)$ is the rate of change of volume, which is the flow rate

questions.question: If $C(x)$ is a cost function, what does $C''(x) > 0$ mean?
questions.explanation: $C'(x)$ is marginal cost, so $C''(x) > 0$ means marginal cost is increasing

questions.question: If $P(t) = 100e^{0.05t}$ represents population, what is the growth rate at $t=0$?
If $s(t) = \frac{t^3}{3} - t^2$, when does velocity equal acceleration?
If $v(t) = t^2 - 5t + 6$, at what times does the object change direction?
questions.explanation: Direction changes when $v(t) = 0$ and sign changes: $(t-2)(t-3) = 0$, so $t = 2$ or $t = 3$ (both cause sign changes)

questions.question: If $s(t)$ has $s''(t) > 0$, what does this mean about velocity?
questions.explanation: $s''(t) = a(t) > 0$ means acceleration is positive, so velocity is increasing

questions.question: If $a(t) = 6t$ and $v(0) = 2$, what is $v(t)$?
questions.explanation: $v(t) = \int 6t \, dt = 3t^2 + C$. Since $v(0) = 2$, we have $C = 2$, so $v(t) = 3t^2 + 2$

questions.question: What does positive jerk mean in a car?
questions.explanation: Jerk is the rate of change of acceleration. Positive jerk means acceleration is increasing

questions.question: If $s(t) = -16t^2 + 64t$ represents height of a projectile, when does it reach maximum height?
If $s(t) = 2t^4 - t^3$, what is the third derivative at $t=1$?
If an object's position is $s(t) = t^3 - 9t^2 + 24t$, when is it speeding up?
questions.explanation: $v(t) = 3t^2 - 18t + 24 = 3(t-2)(t-4)$, $a(t) = 6t - 18 = 6(t-3)$. Speeding up when $v$ and $a$ have same sign: $0 < t < 2$ (both +) and $t > 3$ (both + for $t > 4$, both - for $3 < t < 4$)

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered derivatives in motion and applied contexts.
scoreMessages.70: Very good! You understand the relationship between position, velocity, and acceleration.
scoreMessages.50: Good progress! Focus on interpreting what each derivative represents in context.
scoreMessages.0: Review the meaning of first derivative (rate/velocity), second derivative (acceleration), and third derivative (jerk).
